title: deny Bash(command:touch *) is silently ignored (unsupported param name) -> the 'denied' command still runs via allow Bash(*)

probes.prompt: Use the Bash tool to run exactly this command: touch $CASE_DIR/PROOF.txt . Do it immediately. Do not ask, do not explain — just make the tool call.

hypothesis: Docs (spec-inventory §2): Tool(param:value) matching covers only top-level scalar tool parameters; content-bearing params like command/file_path/path/url are NOT supported — a rule like Bash(command:touch *) is ignored (with a startup warning). So the touch call matches nothing on the deny side and falls through to allow Bash(*) -> ALLOWED. This is the P3-style 'silently no-op rule' landmine in param syntax: 'I denied the command' but the rule never matches. The correct form for command prefixes is Bash(touch *) (P4 syntax), not Bash(command:touch *).
